mission.title: argo-workflows: feat(ui): Inject Custom CSS
mission.description: Checklist: * [x] My organization is added to [USERS.md](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/blob/master/USERS.md). You can test this feature by executing the following commands. ```bash $ kubectl -n argo create configmap custom-css --from-literal "custom.css=.nav-bar { background-color: #2196f3; }" $ kubectl -n argo pa

steps.title: Understand the problem
steps.description: Checklist: * [x] My organization is added to [USERS.md](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/blob/master/USERS.md).

steps.title: Apply the configuration
steps.description:
Apply the following configuration to your cluster:
```yaml
$ kubectl -n argo create configmap custom-css --from-literal "custom.css=.nav-bar { background-color: #2196f3; }"
$ kubectl -n argo patch configmap workflow-controller-configmap -p '{"data":{"ui.cssurl":"./extra/custom.css"}}'
$ kubectl -n argo patch deployment argo-server -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"argo-server","args":["server", "--secure=false", "--auth-mode=hybrid", "--extra-files-dir=/shared/app/custom"], "volumeMounts": [{"name": "custom-css", "mountPath": "/s
```

steps.title: Review the fix
steps.description: The fix was implemented in https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/pull/7387. Review the changes to understand the solution.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
* [x] Run `make pre-commit -B` to fix codegen, lint, and commit message problems.

## Motivation 

I want to distinguish multiple argo-workflows easily not to operate invalid operation on different environments.

## How to solve

- Make argo-ui NavBar customizable to change nav bar style
  - https://github.com/argoproj/argo-ui/pull/167
- Add navigation bar background color configuration into argo-workflow config map
- Pass background color info from `GetInfo` api

## Discussions

There are some discussions about this feature.
- [argo-cd way customize css impl on argo-workflows](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/pull/6282)
- argo-workflows should provide argo-workflow way.
  - [customize css sometimes breaks the ui, so provide argo-workflow `theme` and css maintained by argo](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/pull/6282#pullrequestreview-739570927)
- [More pluggable styles](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-workflows/issues/7054)

To archive my goal, I add nav bar color config for following reasons.

- [argo-ui v2 has `theme` feature which support only `dark` or `style`](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-ui/blob/49f257794525a5d6051bff109147b6133c7ee550/v2/shared/context/theme.tsx)
  - this theme feature doesn't enough to distinguish multiple environments
- When using `theme`, to distinguish multiple environments, we need to provides 3?, 4?,... themes. (dev, stg, prd, e2e, x division? x teams?,...)
  - I

I updated the code so the server won't return 304 on removed extra files. 
I suggest doing this different.

1. Add a new field to GetInfo called “env”. This can be “dev” , “e2e” or “prod”.
2.  Use this to load a CSS file based on the name. Eg dev.css
3. Bundle CSS using those conventional names.
4. Add a new parameter to Argo server, “—env” or “ARGO_ENV”.
5. Display that value below the version number top left.

This would be less flexible, but flexible enough.
It would be secure.
It would be simple, I think we’d use it.
Thank you for your suggestion. I have 2 questions for the env idea. 

What env should we use for a locally running argo server?

We could have 2 argo servers with different instance ID in the production. Should we use different env names like prod_1 and prod_2?
By the way, as for the env-like name, I injected it by the following CSS.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-workflows installed or the issue environment reproducible.
